text: I prefer dark-mode editor themes with a high-contrast monospace font like JetBrains Mono.
My primary keyboard is a Keychron K2 with brown switches; the home-row layout is QWERTZ.
I work from Zurich on weekdays and from a small office in Vorarlberg on weekends.
My coffee setup is a Eureka Mignon grinder with a Lelit Anna espresso machine.
I drink roughly two double espressos in the morning and a long black after lunch.
I read technical articles in English and personal essays in German.
My favorite editor is Neovim with a heavily customized config using lazy.nvim.
I track personal finances with YNAB and review the monthly summary on the first weekend of every month.
I travel mainly to Lisbon, Berlin, and Stockholm, with at least one trip per quarter.
My partner's name is Alma, a literature teacher in Zurich.
I run roughly 30 kilometers per week along the Sihl river path.
I follow a vegetarian diet on weekdays and eat fish on weekends.
I sleep best with the bedroom temperature around 17 degrees Celsius.
I keep my passport stored in the small safe in my home office.
My passport expires in March 2029 and the renewal needs to be filed at the Zurich passport office.
My most-used password manager is 1Password with a dedicated work vault and a personal vault.
I attend a weekly bouldering session at the Milandia gym every Thursday evening.
My preferred airline for European trips is Swiss, mostly for the Geneva-Zurich convenience.
I keep all personal photos in Apple Photos with a 2 TB iCloud plan.
I read about one technical book per month, currently Designing Data-Intensive Applications.
To plan my week, I open the Notion weekly template every Sunday evening and copy the previous week's open items.
To prepare a presentation, I draft an outline in Markdown first, then move it into Keynote only at the end.
To get into deep-work mode, I close Slack, set the Pomodoro timer to 50 minutes, and put on lo-fi instrumentals.
To debug a tricky issue, I write the failing case as a vitest test first, then iterate from there.
To plan a trip, I block dates on the family calendar, book flights via Google Flights, and lodging via Booking.com.
To review long PRs, I check out the branch locally and use vim's :Gdiff before commenting in the GitHub UI.
To prepare for a long run, I drink 500 ml of water and eat a small banana about 45 minutes beforehand.
To handle email, I batch-process the inbox in two 20-minute windows, once after lunch and once before close of day.
My current 5k personal best is 22 minutes and 18 seconds, set in October 2025.
I am currently learning Italian, three short Duolingo sessions per day.
I am two weeks into a new sleep schedule of lights-out by 23:00 on weekdays.
I currently have €4,200 set aside in the emergency fund, on track for the year-end target of €5,000.
I owe a thank-you note to my mentor Hilde for the November coffee chat.
I learned that batching errands into a single weekday evening saved me a full Saturday morning every week.
I found that a 20-minute walk after lunch made the afternoon focus block dramatically more productive.
I learned that writing my daily todos by hand instead of in an app made me actually finish them.
I found that swapping the late-night podcast for ten pages of fiction improved my sleep onset noticeably.
I learned that cycling to the office instead of driving cut my commute stress and added a built-in workout.
I learned that asking explicit clarifying questions at the start of a meeting saved 30 minutes downstream on average.
I learned that capping the work day at 18:00 strictly made the next morning's deep-work block far more effective.
